title: Amazon's Bezos pushes Prime Video redesign focused on AI
description: Jeff Bezos has identified a new, high-profile platform to help showcase the hundreds of billions of dollars Amazon has bet on artificial intelligence: Prime Video.
content:
The Amazon founder and executive chairman pushed Prime Video head Mike Hopkins to overhaul the streaming service so that AI is front and ​center, according to four people with direct knowledge of the matter.

title: Illinois ordered an audit of AI developers. Who will do it?
description: Illinois' statute on artificial intelligence safety requires an audit and, in the same breath, describes a profession that does not exist.
content: On July 6, Gov. JB Pritzker signed the Artificial Intelligence Safety Measures Act, which passed with overwhelming legislative support in the General Assembly. Anthropic backed it. So did OpenAI. The companies being regulated stood alongside the legi... [4892 chars]

title: Zuckerberg Says AI Should Empower People, Not Replace Them
description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg said artificial intelligence should empower people rather than replace them, rejecting what he called a dystopian vision of AI.
content:
Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg unveiled the company's latest vision for artificial intelligence Wednesday, arguing the technology should empower people rather than replace them.
The social media giant released a new video outlining its approach to AI, cont... [2016 chars]

title: Firmus tries to fast track SA data centres as Tassie plans stall
description: A Tasmanian council has postponed its decision on Firmus’ proposal to build an artificial intelligence factory in Bell Bay as it tackles community concerns.
content:
Firmus wants to fast-track billions of dollars of data centre projects in South Australia with state government support, as its expansion plans in Tasmania hit local community resistance and council delays.
The group co-founded by Oliver Curtis and h... [274 chars]

title: CEP IIT Delhi Announces the Launch of Batch 2 of its Executive Programme for AI in Healthcare
description: New Delhi [India], July 23: The Indian Institute of Technology Delhi (IIT Delhi), through its Continuing Education Programme (CEP), has announced Batch 2 of the Executive Programme for AI in Healthcare, following the successful completion of the first cohort. The six-month online executive programme is designed to help working professionals & graduates build applied capabilities in artificial intelligence for healthcare, with a focus on clinical data, medical imaging, predictive analytics, healthcare automation, public health analytics & AI-enabled decision support.
